what: Add tl.assume plus tl.max_contiguous hints to the v211 baseline fused quant kernel
why: v253 showed pure tl.assume was close enough to baseline to stay in play, and the remaining low-risk compiler-guidance step is to additionally mark the hot N-lane vectors as contiguous so Triton can simplify address generation on both loads and packed fp4 stores without changing launch geometry or cache policy.

what: Keep the v211 baseline and add tl.max_contiguous hints only in the fused quant kernel
why: The combined assume-plus-contiguous branch is in flight, but v253 suggests the assume set may be the risky part. Marking only the hot N-lane vectors as contiguous preserves geometry and cache policy while isolating the cheaper address-generation hint on quant loads and packed fp4 stores.

what: Use REDUCE_BSN=16 on the exact v211 baseline split-K path
why: The prior reduce-width experiment was confounded by an accidental M=64 num_warps=2 change, so the baseline-vs-reduce tradeoff was never measured cleanly. This isolates the split-K reduction tile on the known-good geometry while keeping every fused path identical to v211.

what: Use waves_per_eu=4 only for the M=64 fused path on the v211 baseline
why: The remaining large contributor after the split-K shape is 64x7168x2048. waves_per_eu=4 was previously only local-BM neutral, never measured through the current async test-and-benchmark loop on the v211-derived baseline with M<=32 cache=None. This isolates one unspent occupancy point without changing tile sizes or cache policy.

what: Use exact MAX_KSPLIT for the baseline split-K reduce instead of padding to the next power of two
why: The 7-way split-K path currently pads the reduction extent to 8 lanes even though only 7 partials are real. This keeps the winning v211 geometry unchanged and only removes one dead lane from the split-K reduction metadata, which is a cleaner baseline-only follow-up than the REDUCE_BSN experiment that just regressed.

what: Use GROUP_SIZE_M=2 only for the M=64 fused path on the v211 baseline
why: Earlier M=64 group-size tuning was only slightly negative to neutral on older baselines, before the v211 M<=32 cache change. This keeps tile sizes, warp count, and cache policy fixed and only pairs the two 64-row fused M-tiles to test whether B-tile reuse helps enough on the current baseline to offset the scheduling cost.
override: Prior GROUP_SIZE_M=2 data came from pre-v211 baselines and from a bad three-way combination. This run isolates GROUP_SIZE_M=2 on the current active baseline without the confounding cache and occupancy changes.

what: Keep the v258 max-contiguous baseline and add tl.multiple_of hints for the fused quant path strides
why: v258 was the closest BM result to baseline among recent branches. The remaining nearby compiler-guidance step is to keep the successful max_contiguous-only shape and add alignment facts on the bf16 row stride and packed fp4 row stride, without reintroducing the broader tl.assume set that regressed in v257/v253.

what: Keep the v263 max-contiguous plus stride-multiple baseline and add tl.multiple_of on the packed fp4 output lane vector
why: v263 is the closest recent BM result to the baseline and the only non-regressing branch in the current line. The next local extension is to keep the same stride facts and also mark the packed fp4 store vector width as tile-aligned, which may help Triton simplify the packed-output addressing without reintroducing the broader tl.assume path.

what: Restore the v258 tl.max_contiguous hints and keep the v263 stride-multiple hints together in one fused quant branch
why: Recent branch archaeology showed v263/v264 accidentally dropped the tl.max_contiguous lines from v258, so the intended combined compiler-guidance branch was never measured. This candidate is the clean union of the two least-bad hint families: contiguous N-lane vectors plus row-stride alignment facts, without the broader tl.assume set.
override: This overlaps with recent compiler-hint work, but the exact combination has not actually been run because the prior branch drifted and lost the max_contiguous hints.

what: Keep the v263 stride-multiple baseline and add tl.max_contiguous only for the input load lane vector
why: v263 (stride multiples only) was the best recent BM, v258 (both max_contiguous hints) was slightly worse, and v265 (max_contiguous plus stride multiples) regressed further. That suggests the store-side contiguity hint may be the harmful part. This isolates only the load-side x_offs_n contiguity hint on top of the best recent compiler-guidance branch.
